2603.25422.title: Navigating the Prompt Space: Improving LLM Classification of Social Science Texts Through Prompt Engineering
2603.25422.paper_summary: Recent developments in text classification using Large Language Models (LLMs) in the social sciences suggest that costs can be cut significantly, while performance can sometimes rival existing computational methods. However, with a wide variance in performance in current tests, we move to the question of how to maximize performance. In this paper, we focus on prompt context as a possible avenue for increasing accuracy by systematically varying three aspects of prompt engineering: label descriptions, instructional nudges, and few shot examples. Across two different examples, our tests illustrate that a minimal increase in prompt context yields the highest increase in performance, while further increases in context only tend to yield marginal performance increases thereafter. Alarmingly, increasing prompt context sometimes decreases accuracy. Furthermore, our tests suggest substantial heterogeneity across models, tasks, and batch size, underlining the need for individual validation of each LLM coding task rather than reliance on general rules.
2603.25422.paper_summary_zh: 最近在社會科學領域中使用大型語言模型（LLMs）進行文本分類的發展表明，成本可以顯著降低，而性能有時可以與現有的計算方法相媲美。<lb/>然而，鑒於目前測試中性能的廣泛變異，我們轉向如何最大化性能的問題。<lb/>在本文中，我們專注於提示上下文作為提高準確性的可能途徑，通過系統性地變化提示工程的三個方面：標籤描述、指導性提示和少量示例。<lb/>在兩個不同的示例中，我們的測試表明，提示上下文的最小增加會帶來性能的最大提升，而隨後進一步增加上下文往往只會帶來邊際性能的提升。<lb/>令人擔憂的是，增加提示上下文有時會降低準確性。<lb/>此外，我們的測試表明，不同模型、任務和批量大小之間存在顯著的異質性，強調了對每個LLM編碼任務進行個別驗證的必要性，而不是依賴一般規則。
2603.25422.author: Erkan Gunes et.al.
2603.25422.authors: Erkan Gunes, Christoffer Florczak, Tevfik Murat Yildirim
